goals.name: Frontier Synthesis
goals.objective: Build a high-capability synthesis model using frontier models, aiming long term to exceed Fable 5 / Mythos 5 across broad benchmarks.
next_experiments: Add google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview to the existing 10-question LitQA2 microscope.
Compare best single, majority, weighted vote, default Synth, disagreement-only judge/synth, and debate-then-synth.
success_criteria: Beat best single model and default Synth on paired rows.
Show positive paired bootstrap or McNemar signal on 50+ rows.
Eventually beat Fable 5 / Mythos 5 baselines across a broad suite once those baselines are available in the same harness.

goals.name: OS Pareto Synthesis
goals.objective: Build an open-source/open-weight-only synthesis model that beats GLM 5.2 and Kimi K2.6 while costing far less than GPT-5.5.
next_experiments: Run open-only 7-model panel on 20 LitQA2 questions.
Compute pairwise agreement, correctness correlation, unique-correct counts, rescue matrix, oracle gain, majority vote, and weighted vote.
Only after analytics, run minority-defense/debate on disagreement rows.
success_criteria: Beat GLM 5.2 and Kimi K2.6 on paired rows.
Beat simple majority and best single open model on 20-50 item pilots.
Demonstrate better accuracy/cost than GPT-5.5 on at least one benchmark slice.
Preserve easy consensus rows through early exit.

shared_principles: Use the first 10 LitQA2 questions only as a debugging microscope, not a proof.
Save raw panel outputs for offline aggregation sweeps.
Track accuracy, latency, token cost, provider errors, and no-answer rate together.
Prefer adaptive escalation over always-on expensive debate.
Separate judge and final synthesis roles unless testing same-model collapse.
Measure panel oracle before judging process quality.
